file_insights.key_content: Package-level documentation describing all modular Django applications (core, menu, orders, subscriptions, customers, media, notifications, analytics, ai)
file_insights.significance: HIGH - Establishes the apps package structure and documents the architecture for all future Django applications

file_insights.changes_made: Created new core app initialization with default_app_config
file_insights.key_content: Critical rules documentation: tenant isolation via organizationId, soft delete pattern with deleted_at timestamp, no physical deletions
file_insights.significance: CRITICAL - Sets foundational constraints and patterns for the entire core application

file_insights.changes_made: Created with placeholder documentation and commented examples
file_insights.key_content: Template admin classes for Organization and User models with multi-tenant filtering patterns, soft delete handling, and fieldset organization
file_insights.significance: HIGH - Provides ready-to-implement admin interfaces with best practices for multi-tenant admin functionality

file_insights.changes_made: Created CoreConfig class with full configuration
file_insights.key_content: AppConfig with BigAutoField default, verbose name 'Core', signal registration ready hook (commented)
file_insights.significance: CRITICAL - Establishes proper Django app configuration and provides hook for future signal registration

patterns_discovered.description: Every database query must include organizationId for tenant isolation; critical rule documented in core/__init__.py
patterns_discovered.implications: All future models and queries must enforce this constraint; admin filters must apply tenant filtering

patterns_discovered.description: All entities use soft delete with deleted_at timestamp instead of physical deletion
patterns_discovered.implications: Must filter deleted_at__isnull=True in all querysets; no physical deletions allowed in the system

patterns_discovered.description: All files include detailed docstrings explaining purpose, constraints, and patterns
patterns_discovered.implications: Establishes expectation for high documentation standards throughout the codebase

patterns_discovered.description: Admin classes are provided as commented examples/templates rather than fully implemented
patterns_discovered.implications: Allows flexibility in admin implementation while providing reference patterns for future development

gotchas_discovered.description: CoreConfig.name must be 'apps.core' (not just 'core') to match the import path; default_app_config in __init__.py must also match
gotchas_discovered.impact: Incorrect path will cause Django app loading failures
gotchas_discovered.mitigation: Verified correct path used: 'apps.core' in both apps.py and __init__.py

gotchas_discovered.description: Admin classes and all views must explicitly filter deleted_at__isnull=True in get_queryset() to avoid showing deleted records
gotchas_discovered.impact: Deleted records could appear in admin interface if filtering is omitted
gotchas_discovered.mitigation: Template includes example get_queryset() implementation with soft delete filtering

gotchas_discovered.description: Signals must be imported in ready() method with try/except to handle import errors gracefully
gotchas_discovered.impact: Improper signal registration can cause app loading failures
gotchas_discovered.mitigation: Template provided with commented example showing correct pattern

gotchas_discovered.description: Must explicitly set default_auto_field = 'django.db.models.BigAutoField' to avoid Django 3.2+ warnings
gotchas_discovered.impact: Django deprecation warnings if not set; good for future-proofing

approach_outcome.summary: Successfully created the core Django app structure with AppConfig and established foundational architectural patterns
tasks_completed: Created e_menum/apps/__init__.py with package documentation describing all planned applications
Created e_menum/apps/core/__init__.py with critical rules and default_app_config
Created e_menum/apps/core/admin.py with template admin classes for future model admin registration
Created e_menum/apps/core/apps.py with proper CoreConfig implementation including signal hook
key_achievements: Established multi-tenant architecture constraints in documentation
Defined soft delete pattern as foundational pattern
Provided template implementations for admin interfaces
Set up proper Django app configuration with BigAutoField default
Created infrastructure for signal registration in ready() method
approach_outcome.time_efficiency: First attempt successful - no rework needed

recommendations.recommendation: Implement core models (Organization, User, Role, Permission, Session, Branch, AuditLog) following the patterns documented in core/__init__.py
recommendations.rationale: Core models are foundational; all other apps depend on multi-tenant and soft-delete patterns

recommendations.recommendation: Create a mixin or base model class that enforces soft delete pattern (deleted_at field and default queryset filtering)
recommendations.rationale: Will reduce boilerplate across all models and ensure consistent soft delete implementation

recommendations.recommendation: Add tests for tenant isolation to verify organizationId filtering works correctly in queries
recommendations.rationale: Multi-tenant isolation is security-critical; must be thoroughly tested

recommendations.recommendation: Create a ARCHITECTURE.md document explaining the multi-tenant design, soft delete pattern, and RBAC structure
recommendations.rationale: Will help future developers understand these critical constraints

recommendations.recommendation: Create signals.py in core app and uncomment signal import in CoreConfig.ready() once models are implemented
recommendations.rationale: Signals module exists but is currently disabled; implement when core models are created

recommendations.recommendation: Implement admin classes from templates in admin.py once models are created, ensuring soft delete filtering is applied
recommendations.rationale: Templates are ready; just need model implementations and queryset filtering
